title: MAGI Agentic AI Engineering Platform
summary: Internal FastAPI platform for advisory, explainable, read-only analysis over CMMI Appraisal System data, built around bounded agentic AI workflows, code context, SQL validation, Azure OpenAI, and Ukrainian/Eastern Europe delivery coordination.
impact: Led MAGI as an agentic AI platform that combines Azure SQL schema discovery, Azure DevOps source context, Azure OpenAI, SQL validation, and bounded execution for explainable appraisal-system analysis.
Designed the deployment architecture across Azure App Service, Azure SQL, Azure AI Search, Azure OpenAI/APIM, Key Vault, ACR, managed identity, SAML/Kentico role access, and Azure DevOps pipelines.
Coordinated internal engineers with Ukrainian/Eastern Europe-based offshore contributors across architecture, review, implementation, and agile delivery.
Kept the AI workflow advisory and read-only by design, with explicit validation and execution boundaries instead of unbounded automation.

evidence.label: Delivery model
evidence.value: Internal ISACA platform led with Ukrainian/Eastern Europe-based offshore coordination.

evidence.label: System frame
evidence.value: Agentic AI framed as bounded analysis, retrieval, code context, schema discovery, and SQL-validation infrastructure rather than chat-only prompting.

title: Early Generative AI Workflow Prototypes
summary: Python prototypes from September-November 2023 showing GPT-3.5 integrated with browser automation, OAuth-backed email APIs, local workflow state, and PostgreSQL records.
impact: Built practical LLM-backed automation in 2023 using GPT-3.5 as one component inside a larger workflow rather than as a standalone chat demo.
Connected Python, Playwright browser control, OAuth/Gmail API access, local state files, and PostgreSQL-backed records into repeatable private prototypes.
Used extracted page context, prompt boundaries, generated short task-specific text, and programmatic UI actions to test how AI could fit into workflow automation.
Kept the private context out of the public portfolio while keeping the engineering pattern clear: model output, workflow state, API access, and browser actions working together.

evidence.label: Timeframe
evidence.value: Core Python prototypes created and modified across September-November 2023.

evidence.label: Technical pattern
evidence.value: LLM-generated task text, browser automation, OAuth-backed email API access, local JSON state, and PostgreSQL workflow records.

evidence.label: Privacy boundary
evidence.value: Private context and secrets are intentionally excluded.

title: Destro Machines SwimView Platform
summary: Swim View and supporting software for pool-deck performance data: web application, mobile tooling, BLE device integration, cloud backend, analytics workflows, and Mexican engineering collaboration.
impact: Led a three-engineer effort with Mexican engineering collaboration to build Swim View, a .NET, C#, React, and Redux application that visualizes data gathered from swimming pool decks via Swim Tower products.
Developed BLE accessory firmware in C, iPhone and iPad application work, App Engine backend services, and d3.js visualization for swimming smart-meter data.
Designed a high-precision BLE time synchronization mechanism using drift and offset bounding to align iPhone/iPad clocks with device time.
Transitioned the project cleanly to another developer after completing the core software contributions.

title: Commerce, Fulfillment, and Payment Systems
summary: Destro Machines commerce work: custom Go packing logic, Stripe and UPS checkout integration, Shopify storefront work, Mexican engineering collaboration, and payment-platform research around Sylius and PayPal.
impact: Built a custom Go ecommerce platform for Destro Machines that connected payment, fulfillment, and shipping decisions into one product purchase flow.
Authored boxpacker, a Go implementation of 3D bin-packing and shipment optimization logic used to reason about package selection, dimensional constraints, and UPS fulfillment costs.
Implemented Shopify storefront work through the Destro-Machines/dmshopify repository, including Dawn-derived theme updates and storefront styling integration.

Worked through payment ecosystem details using PayPalPlugin, Sylius, Payum, php-ups-api, Guzzle, and SyliusDocker forks as supporting research for checkout, refund, shipping, and platform integration decisions.

evidence.label: Contribution
evidence.value: Commerce and integration work connected to Destro Machines, with ecosystem forks and research treated as supporting context.

title: Embedded Recovery and Sensor Validation Lab
summary: Hardware-adjacent engineering around board recovery, Nordic quadrature-decoder validation, angular velocity calculation, BLE-adjacent device work, and practical field debugging.
impact: Published arduino-nano-33-iot-unbrick, a documented SWD bootloader recovery path using an Arduino programmer, SD-card firmware loading, and direct Nano 33 IoT recovery wiring.
Built nrf_qdec_tester around Nordic SDK QDEC behavior, report periods, sample periods, pulse windows, and angular velocity calculations for sensor validation.
Used qdectest and related hardware experiments to validate low-level device behavior rather than relying only on polished application work.
Shows the device-level debugging and sensor reasoning behind the polished product work.

evidence.label: Contribution
evidence.value: Embedded investigation, recovery workflow documentation, and product-adjacent hardware debugging.

impact: Implemented interactive campaign surfaces from design and motion direction.
Built canvas and JavaScript behavior for embedded retail experiences.
Maintained video/audio-heavy UI in brand campaign contexts.

impact: Built rich product simulators and supporting campaign UI.

title: GigDig iPhone App
summary: Front-end implementation work for an iPhone application with a screen-heavy product flow and custom interface design.
impact: Implemented mobile UI surfaces from designed screens.

summary: Interactive comparison and campaign pieces built from prototype animation, supplied designs, and product imagery.

title: Healthy Sight Fund Panorama Work
summary: A Flash-era Papervision panorama and vision-simulation module rebuilt as a modern JavaScript experience from original interactive assets.
impact: Built immersive panorama-style interactions from supplied media and Papervision 3D scene files.
Recreated mouse-driven camera easing, scene changes, foreground planes, and eye-health overlay effects in JavaScript.
Worked through the constraints of browser plug-ins, video assets, and immersive interaction patterns.

evidence.label: Original assets
evidence.value: FLA, SWF, ActionScript, Papervision, Collada, and image assets

evidence.label: Scene assets

title: Post-Gazette Truck Delivery Manifest System
role: C# reporting and logistics systems engineer
summary: A truck delivery manifest and reporting system for Post-Gazette newspaper distribution operations, with generated output spanning hundreds of printable report pages.
impact: Built the C# / ASP.NET reporting workflow used in Post-Gazette distribution operations.

Worked with manifest generation, depot and dispatch views, pallet sheets, route detail records, draw totals, CSV translation files, and operational report output large enough to generate hundreds of printable pages.
Used T4 templates and typed record parsers to generate multiple report surfaces from distribution data.
Represents logistics software work outside campaign/front-end projects: operational data, reporting, and delivery workflow support.

evidence.label: Report scale
evidence.value: Manifest visual derived from a 21.5 MB generated Manifest.html: 2,717 page-break markers, 121,690 rows, and street-level route details redacted for public display.

evidence.label: Contribution
evidence.value: A specific logistics/reporting system for the Post-Gazette, not a generic code sample.

title: ServiceLink Call Center Application
summary: A Level Agency-era .NET MVC system used by a roughly 55-person ServiceLink call center. I was dropped into the existing application to diagnose and improve severe performance problems under real operational load.
impact: Inherited an existing ASP.NET MVC / Entity Framework application whose database was being overwhelmed by call-center usage.
Analyzed workflows around categorized knowledge content, discussions, content templates, content items, assets, permissions, printers, sharing, upload handling, and searchable management screens.
Diagnosed the core performance pattern: lazy-loaded EF entities, unbounded admin queries, and view-layer relationship traversal producing excessive SQL traffic during normal agent workflows.
Used the existing codebase to identify what the business needed while avoiding the architecture patterns that made the system slow.

evidence.label: Contribution
evidence.value: Presented as inherited legacy-system diagnosis and performance stabilization work, not as original authorship or a modern architecture pattern.
